title: BlackRock's Secret Bitcoin Move
transcript: Bitcoin just hit a weekly RSI of 25.6. That is  not a typo. That is the most oversold reading   in the entire 17-year history of this asset. And  every mainstream financial headline right now is   pointing to the same conclusion. Institutional  money is abandoning Bitcoin. They will site   4.5 billion in net outflows from US spot Bitcoin  ETFs since January. They will tell you that Black   Rockck's IBIT has shed 2.1 billion in 5 weeks.  They will show you a fear and greed index at   levels so low that they've only appeared a handful  of times in crypto's history. The story writes   itself. Wall Street came, Wall Street saw, and now  Wall Street is leaving. Except on February 26th,   while those outflow headlines were still printing,  blockchain trackers flagged something that   completely shattered that narrative. Black Rockck  executed a $289.6 million spot Bitcoin purchase in   under one hour. The very same week IBIT was making  outflow headlines, the onchain data showed $4,39   bitcoin moving from Coinbase Prime directly into  IBIT custody wallets in a series of coordinated   transfers spaced minutes apart. So, either the  biggest asset manager on the planet has no idea   what its own fund is doing, or the story you're  being told about institutional abandonment is   dangerously incomplete. My name is Lewis and  you're watching the Coin Bureau. So, is Black   Rockck genuinely playing both sides of the Bitcoin  market? Is the ETF outflow data a misleading   signal that is actively engineering panic among  retail investors? or is there a structural   explanation for this divergence that most  headlines are completely missing? That is exactly   what we're going to be diving into today. First  things first though, let's look at the data that   has the retail market absolutely terrified right  now. Since the start of 2026, US spot Bitcoin ETFs   have logged nearly 4.5 billion in commulative  net outflows. The 5we outflow streak alone,   the longest since February 2025, counted for  approximately 3.8 8 billion of that figure. Black   Rockck's IBIT led the bleed, shedding roughly $2.1  billion. Fidelity's FBTC saw over 954 million walk   out the door. Meanwhile, Bitcoin's price has  fallen 47.6% from its all-time high of $126,296 that it reached in October of 2025. And on  February 6th, the crypto fear and greed index   hit a reading of five out of 100. Yes, five.  To put that into context, the index bottomed   at six during the FTX collapse. Sentiment right  now is worse than what it was in the wake of   one of crypto's most catastrophic institutional  failures. Now, when algorithms see ETF outflows   of this magnitude, they do exactly what they're  programmed to do. They interpret the data as a   signal that institutional demand is dead and  momentum traders pile in on the short side.   Retail investors watching their portfolios bleed  red capitulate into a market already gripped by   maximum fear and the machine feeds itself.  The mainstream narrative is clean, simple,   and emotionally compelling. Problem is that  it is only looking at half the picture. But   here is where the story turns. On February 26th,  blockchain tracking platform Arkham Intelligence,   first flagged by Look on Chain, tracked something  that the ETF Outflow headlines completely missed.   In the space of approximately 1 hour, beginning  around 5:45 p.m. UTC, Black Rockck's IBIT wallet   received 4,39 Bitcoin from Coinbase Prime. The  transfers were structured as repeated blocks   of roughly 300 BTC each with two transactions  occurring within the same minute and others spaced   3 to four minutes apart. Total value at execution  $289.6 million. This was not a rumor and it was   not speculation. This was onchain data verifiable  on the public ledger traceable to wallet addresses   Arkham has attributed to the Eyesshar's Bitcoin  trust. And then the very next day, February 27th,   the same day that IBIT recorded a $32.99 million  outflow, which was its largest daily outflow   during that specific late February episode,  onchain data showed approximately 2,563 BTC and   49,852 ETH moving in the other direction from iBit  wallets back into Coinbase Prime. Total value of   that transfer, well, approximately 270 million.  same week, same institution, one flow going in,   one flow going out. And virtually nobody in the  mainstream press connected the dots between them.   Now, this is the point where you might be  tempted to reach for the word manipulation,   an understandable impulse. But that  framing, as satisfying as it feels,   is actually less interesting and less useful  than understanding what is really going on   here. Because the truth is simultaneously  more mundane and more consequential.   But before we get into that, did you know that  if you use Coinbase Advanced, you could actually   trade with fees as low as 0%. This is built in  right into the Coinbase app. No separate account,   no extra download, just one click to unlock. And  once you're in, maker fees can go as low as 0%.   And if you're coming from another exchange, their  VIP status match lets you upload proof of your   trading volume and immediately unlock a lower fee  tier for 60 days. So you don't have to start from   zero. That sounds pretty good to me. But now let's  get back to BlackRock. To understand what Black   Rockck is actually doing, you have to understand  the plumbing that sits underneath the ETF system.   Most retail investors have no idea this  plumbing exists, and the firms that built   it are not exactly rushing to explain it. Here  is how Bitcoin ETFs work at the institutional   level. Black Rockck does not buy or sell Bitcoin  directly from your brokerage account. Instead, a   set of specially licensed firms called authorized  participants or APS sit between the public market   and the ETF itself. For IBIT, those APs include  names like Jane Street, Virtue Americas, JP Morgan   Securities, and Marx. These are the only entities  on Earth allowed to create or redeem IBIT shares   directly with BlackRock. When retail investors  or smaller institutions sell their IBIT shares   on the secondary market, those shares can pile up  at a discount to the fund's actual Bitcoin value.   An AP buys those discounted shares, bundles them  into a redemption basket, and presents them to   Black Rockck in exchange for the underlying  Bitcoin. Bitcoin moves some IBIT wallets to   the AP. The AP takes that Bitcoin to Coinbase  Prime. Black Rockck records the day as an outflow.   The headline reads, "IBIT sees 32.99 million  net outflow." Do you see what just happened?   The headline is not wrong, but it's describing  a mechanical contractual settlement process,   not a mass exit of institutional conviction. Now,  layer on top of this, the cash and carry basis   trade, and the picture gets even more complex.  For the last 2 years, a significant percentage   of the capital sitting inside Bitcoin ETFs was  never there because someone believed Bitcoin was   going to go to the moon. It was there because the  price difference between Bitcoin spot and Bitcoin   futures on the CME was generating annualized  yields as high as 15 to 30%. Here's how it   works. An institution buys IBIT shares on the  spot market and simultaneously shorts CME Bitcoin   futures contracts at the higher futures price. The  gap between those two prices is their risk-free   profit. The ETF share is not a conviction bet.  It's inventory in an arbitrage strategy. When CME   futures premiums compress when the spread narrows  or inverts, the entire trade unwinds at once. The   institution redeems its IBIT shares through an AP,  closes its CME future short, and the published ETF   flow data shows this is a massive outflow from  Bitcoin. Amber Data's forensic analysis found   the correlation between basis compression and  ETF outflows sits at 0.878. 878. That is not   a loose association but a near mechanical  relationship. And their research suggests   that between 20 and 35% of all capital that flowed  into Bitcoin ETFs was there for the basis trade,   not directional Bitcoin exposure. And speaking of  tracking these institutional mechanics before they   hit the mainstream headlines, this is exactly why  you need the Coin Bureau Telegram channel in your   life. It is where we share the latest breaking  news, provide deep dive alpha, and give you the   most important market updates, all pinged directly  to your device so you don't miss a thing. Whether   it's onchain whale movements, ETF flow data, or  macro signals flashing before the broader market   reacts, sign up today using the link down below  or by scanning this QR code right over here. Okay,   so we have established that the ETF outflows are  a deeply misleading signal during periods of basis   compression. But here is the harder question that  the flow data is actually masking. Because while   the basis trade mechanics explain the outflow  headline, they do not explain who is accumulating   the Bitcoin that leaves these ETF structures.  And that is where the onchain supply data tells   a completely different story. Exchange reserves of  Bitcoin have fallen to approximately 1.8 8 million   BTC. According to BRN Research head Timothy Miser,  that is the lowest level since 2017. Over 171,000   Bitcoin were withdrawn from major exchanges in  just the weeks following the US election cycle   in late 2025. Meanwhile, Glass Node confirms that  long-term holder supply currently sits at roughly   14.4 4 million BTC, approximately 72.7% of all  Bitcoin in circulation. These are coins that   have not moved in over 155 days. And they are  not moving now. Not into fear, not into panic,   not anywhere. At the same time, crypto analytics  firm Santiment flagged that wallets holding 100   BTC or more were approaching 20,000 in number.  and market analyst Mattie Greenspan confirmed   that accumulator addresses have absorbed  roughly 372,000 BTC since late December.   A rate of accumulation historically associated  with cycle bottoms, not cycle peaks. The most   recent onchain data showed Bitcoin whales net  purchased approximately 270,000 BTC over the past   month alone. At current prices, that is roughly  $23 billion worth of Bitcoin. quietly swept up   while retail panic sold their ETF shares. That is  the largest net whale purchase in over 13 years.   Now step back and hold both of those pictures  simultaneously. On one side, ETF outflow headlines   screaming institutional abandonment. On the other,  onchain accumulation data showing the largest   whale buying event in over a decade. Those two  things are not easily reconcilable if you assume   the headlines tell the whole story. They are  perfectly reconcilable though once you understand   that the capital leaving through the ETF door is  largely basis trade arbitrage unwinding while an   entirely different class of institutional money  is entering through the OTC door invisible to   published flow data. Coinbase Prime processed  hundreds of billions of dollars in institutional   OTC volume in 2025. OTC trading volume surged  109% year-over-year in 2025 compared to just 9%   growth on the top 20 centralized exchanges. None  of that shows up in the ETF flow reports. All of   it shows up on the blockchain if you know where  to look. Now, here is where the supply dynamics   add the final piece. Bitcoin's weekly RSI has  fallen to 25.6. Multiple independent sources   have confirmed this reading with Mattie Greenspan  calling it the lowest RSI reading in Bitcoin's   recorded history. The only comparable weekly  oversold episodes were November and December   of 2018 when Bitcoin was crashing from $6,000 to  $3,000 and June and July 2022 during the three   arrows capital collapse. In both of those cases,  the extreme oversold reading preceded the final   capitulation flush before a structural recovery.  In 2020, after the COVID crash sent Bitcoin to   $3,850 and the fear and greed index hit single  digits, the asset recovered its pre-crash level   in approximately 75 days and closed the year up  nearly 300%. Now the current draw down does have   a critical structural difference from June 2022  which is worth being precise about. In 2022 the   collapse was accompanied by systemic contagion the  implosion of Teral Luna the bankruptcy of Celsius   three arrows capital FTX. One failure triggered  the next. Bitcoin fell 77.6% over 12 months and   took 24 months to reach a new all-time high.  Bernstein Research has explicitly noted that   the current 2026 drawdown lacks those defining  contagion features. No major exchange collapse,   no algorithmic stable coin implosions,  ETF infrastructure fully intact at   84.8 billion in total AUM across the  complex. The 200week moving average,   which has marked the absolute bare market floor in  2015, 2018, and 2022, currently sits at $57,926. Bitcoin has not breached it. In 2022, Bitcoin fell  below that line in June and did not reclaim it for   16 months. The fact that the 200 WMA is holding  as support, combined with the most extreme weekly   RSI in history and whale accumulation at 13-year  highs, is precisely the setup that Wall Street's   institutional desks spend years waiting to  position into. What's the verdict? Well,   to be blunt, the ETF outflow headlines are  lying to you by omission. Yes, the outflows   are real. Yes, 3.8 8 billion leaving the ETF  complex in 5 weeks is historically significant.   But the structural driver of those outflows,  confirmed by Amber Data's correlation analysis,   by CME open interest data, and by Bloomberg's  own ETF analysts, is the mechanical unwinding   of basis trade arbitrage positions, not the mass  exit of long-term believers abandoning Bitcoin's   thesis. The 289.6 6 million spot Bitcoin purchase  on February 26th was real, as were the 270 million   in onchain transfers between IBIT wallets and  Coinbase Prime. And critically, these movements   represent the operational settlement mechanics of  the IBIT trust. Bitcoin flowing between custodial   infrastructure in direct response to creation  and redemption activity, not some clandestine   corporate accumulation Black Rockck is  running with its own proprietary capital.   The intellectual honesty here matters. Black  Rockck is a fiduciary acting on client orders,   not a single entity actor secretly loading  its own balance sheet. But here is what is   happening and it is arguably more significant.  The entities who actually built positions into   their fear are the ones that 13F filings  have confirmed. Sovereign wealth funds like   Abu Dhabi's Mabuala increased their IBIT exposure  during the Q4 2025 drawdown. Approximately 1,690   institutional entities collectively hold IBIT  shares, names including Millennium, Jane Street,   Citadel, and multiple sovereign  linked capital pools. And onchain,   whale wallets have absorbed $270,000 Bitcoin  in the past month alone. Short-term holders,   largely retail and leveraged, have been  realizing losses at nearly 500 million per day.   Glass node confirmed this is a full transition  into excess loss realization regime language last   used during FTX era capitulation that capital  is not disappearing. It is transferring. The   institutional adoption of Bitcoin was never going  to be a straight line up. It was always going to   involve cycles of engineered looking volatility.  Whether that engineering is intentional or just a   mechanical product of basis trade unwinds and  AP redemptions is ultimately less important   than the outcome. The outcome is that patient  capital is accumulating while impatient capital   exits at a loss. Black Rockck is not playing some  villainous double game. But the ETF system they   built and the derivatives infrastructure that  sits underneath of it does create a structure   where outflow headlines consistently overstate  institutional retreat during exactly the periods   when different classes of institutional money are  most aggressively accumulating. That asymmetry of   information is not an accident and retail  investors who read only the headlines are   bearing the cost of it. But what do you think?  Is Black Rockck's ETF infrastructure genuinely   distorting the retail perception of institutional  demand? Or is this just the inevitable complexity   of a maturing asset class with multiple competing  capital pools? Let me know your thoughts in the   comments below. And if you want to understand the  exact mechanics of how the CME basis trade has   reshaped Bitcoin's price discovery, well, you can  check out our deep dive of that right over here.   And if you want to see why the traditional  safe haven narrative is currently failing   its biggest geopolitical test, well, we  have a video on that right over here. Okay,   thank you all so much for watching and I'll see  you again very soon. This is Lewis signing off.
summary_hu: A Bitcoin heti RSI 25.6-ra csökkent, ami rekord alacsony oversold szintet mutat. A mainstream pénzügyi hírek arról számolnak be, hogy az intézményi befektetők elhagyják a Bitcoint a közel 4,5 milliárdos ETF kiáramlás miatt, azonban a BlackRock az IBIT alap keretein belül 289,6 millió dollár értékben vásárolt Bitcoin-t, ami ellentétes a jelenlegi narratívával. Az adatok azt mutatják, hogy míg az ETF-ekből nagy összegű kiáramlás történt, a Bitcoin hálózaton belüli tárolás egyre inkább növekszik, és a bálnák számos Bitcoint vásároltak az árfolyam esése alatt. A kiskereskedelmi befektetők pánikba estek, míg az intézményi szereplők a stratégiai pozícióik kiépítésére összpontosítanak. A kriptovaluták iránti általános félelem ellenére a Bitcoin ETF rendszere alátámasztja, hogy az intézményi kereslet nem tűnt el, csupán átrendeződött.
summary_en: Bitcoin's weekly RSI has fallen to 25.6, marking an all-time low in oversold conditions. Mainstream financial headlines indicate that institutional investors are abandoning Bitcoin due to nearly $4.5 billion in ETF outflows. However, BlackRock executed a $289.6 million Bitcoin purchase within the same timeframe, contradicting the narrative of mass institutional withdrawal. Data reveals that while significant outflows occurred, on-chain holdings are increasingly accumulating, as whales are purchasing Bitcoin during the downturn. Retail investors are panicking, while institutional players are focused on building strategic positions. Despite the general fear in crypto sentiment, the ETF framework suggests that institutional demand has not vanished but rather shifted.
key_points_hu: A BlackRock 289,6 millió dollár értékben vásárolt Bitcoint az ETF kiáramlások félretétele mellett.
A Bitcoin hálózaton belüli bálnák vásárlása rekord magasságba emelkedett a kiskereskedelmi befektetők pánikja alatt.
Az ETF kiáramlások mechanikusan zajlanak, nem jeleznek elhagyási hullámot.
key_points_en: BlackRock purchased $289.6 million worth of Bitcoin amidst ETF outflows.
On-chain whale purchases of Bitcoin have surged during retail investor panic.
ETF outflows are more mechanical settlements than indicators of abandonment.
